data.title: Vitrolife AB (publ) reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Vitrolife AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:VTRLY): Q2  Net income was SEK 100 (143) million, resulting in earnings per share of SEK 0.74 (1.06).
  • Gross margin decreased to 58.0% (59.9) also negatively impacted by currency.
  • Earnings before depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) decreased to SEK 243 (327) million, resulting in an EBITDA margin of 27.8% (34.7), significantly impacted by negative currency effect.
  • Operating cash flow amounted to SEK 151 million (236).
  •

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Abbott Laboratories Q2 beats revenue estimates; narrows FY outlook range
HP Enterprise rises after getting Overweight rating at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
J.P. Morgan has rated Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) Overweight from a Not Rated designation, with a December 2026 price target of $30 on the stock.

Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise rose about 2% premarket on Thursday.

Analysts led by Samik Chatterjee said they have moved to the Overweight rating following the completion of Juniper Networks' acquisition by HPE which enhances its position and firmly establishes it as one of the largest Networking companies, with one of the broadest portfolios encompassing switches, wireless access points, routers, and more.

The analysts added that with the increasing mix of higher margin networking revenues, supplemented by a plethora of cost synergies, they see a pathway for HPE to achieve earnings of at least $2.70 by fiscal 2027, underscoring the significant upside potential for the shares.

However, the analysts noted that recent concerns relative to execution is likely to lead investors to more steadily warm-up to the higher earnings multiple while watching critical milestones on integration.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories falls after setting Q3 outlook below consensus
data.description:
Shares of Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) lost ~5% in the premarket on Thursday after the MedTech company provided its guidance for the third quarter slightly below consensus following a mixed earnings report for Q2 2025.

While the North Chicago, Illinois-based healthcare giant estimated its adjusted diluted earnings per share for Q3 could reach $1.28 - $1.32 compared to $1.33 in the consensus, its full-year outlook for adj. EPS stood at $5.10 - $5.20, in line with $5.16 projected by analysts.

However, the company’s projection for 2025 organic sales growth at 6.0%–7.0%, including COVID-19 testing-related sales, fell short of the ~7.4% estimated by Wall Street analysts, according to Bloomberg data.

As for Q3 financials, Abbott (NYSE:ABT) reported $11.1B in revenue with ~7% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $80M amid a slight underperformance in its Diagnostics division, while other units outperformed.

ABT’s Diagnostics division generated ~$2.17B in sales with a ~1% YoY decline compared to $2.21B in the consensus, as the company’s COVID-19 testing-related sales fell ~16% YoY to $55M, missing the $63.5M mark projected by analysts.

Meanwhile, the company’s Medical devices and Nutrition segments added $5.37B and $2.21B to the topline with ~13% YoY and ~3% YoY growth compared to $5.23B and $2.24B in Street forecasts, respectively.

ABT’s diabetes products added $1.98B to the topline with ~20% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus of $1.93B as sales of continuous glucose monitors generated $1.9B with ~21% YoY growth.

The company recorded $1.38B in sales from its Established pharmaceuticals division with a ~7% YoY growth, while Street forecasts suggested it would record $1.37B in sales for the quarter.

ABT’s adjusted operating margin for the quarter improved 100 bps from a year ago to 22.9%, even as the company’s adj. earnings per share at $1.26 stood in line with the consensus despite ~11% YoY growth. The company projected its full-year adj. operating margin to reach nearly 23.5%.

data.title: Aemetis appoints CFO of India subsidiary Universal Biofuels
data.description:
• Aemetis (NASDAQ:AMTX) announced Anjaneyulu Ganji as chief financial officer of its India subsidiary, Universal Biofuels, effective July 17, 2025.
  • Ganji's experiences include leading Dodla Dairy’s $450M operations and its oversubscribed initial public offering in 2021.
  • Universal Biofuels operates an 80M gallon/year biodiesel and glycerin facility on India’s East Coast.
  • The subsidiary is expanding into ethanol and renewable natural gas to meet rising energy demand in India.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Dynacor Group reports record sales of $30.8M for June 2025
data.description:
• Dynacor Group (TSX:DNG:CA) reported on Thursday record gold sales of $30.8M in June and record quarterly sales of $79.7M. 
  • The increase in sales of $12.1M, or +64.7%, versus June 2024 is due to a higher sales price and an increase in volume. 
  • The company reported a record cumulative gold sales of $159.7 million at the end of June 2025, compared to $135.1M for the same period of 2024. 
  • With an expected stronger second half of the year, the company is on target to meet its annual sales guidance of between $345 and $375 million.

data.title: Dynatrace opens Boston headquarters
data.description:
• Dynatrace (NYSE:DT) has opened a new corporate headquarters at Atlantic Wharf, 280 Congress Street, in the heart of Boston.
  • The office is situated within a 32-story mixed-use tower, recognized as Boston’s first green skyscraper and a certified LEED Platinum building.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cass Information Systems GAAP EPS of $0.66 misses by $0.06, revenue of $44.4M misses by $6.6M
data.description:
• Cass Information Systems press release (NASDAQ:CASS): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.66 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $44.4M (+1.0% Y/Y) misses by $6.6M.
  • Return on average equity and assets of 15.35% and 1.48%, respectively.
  • Increase in net interest margin to 3.78% from 3.32%.
  • Increase in net interest income of $3.5 million, or 22.3%.
